text:
MOLECULAR GENETICS / GERMLINE TESTING REPORT

DEPARTMENT OF LABORATORY GENETICS AND GENOMICS

---

SPECIMEN INFORMATION

Specimen ID: LGG-2024-BRCA-0471
Patient Name: Okonkwo, Renata
Date of Birth: [DOB on file]
Ordering Physician: [Oncology / Medical Genetics]
Indication for Testing: Personal history of triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast, Grade 3 (newly diagnosed, Stage IIB); strong family history of breast and ovarian cancer (mother: breast cancer diagnosed age 42; maternal aunt: ovarian cancer); age at diagnosis 39 years; premenopausal; somatic BRCA1 frameshift mutation (VAF 45%) identified on tumor next-generation sequencing (tumor-only platform), prompting germline evaluation
Specimen Type: Peripheral blood (two 10 mL EDTA tubes)
Method of Collection: Phlebotomy, peripheral venous
Specimen Adequacy: Adequate for analysis
DNA Extraction: High-molecular-weight genomic DNA extracted from peripheral blood leukocytes using standard automated extraction protocol. DNA yield and quality confirmed by spectrophotometry (A260/A280 ratio 1.87) prior to library preparation.
Laboratory Accreditation: CAP-accredited, CLIA-certified molecular genetics laboratory
Testing Platform: Next-generation sequencing (NGS)-based comprehensive hereditary cancer panel with reflex Sanger sequencing confirmation of all pathogenic and likely pathogenic variants. Large rearrangement/deletion analysis performed by multiplex ligation-dependent probe amplification (MLPA) for all genes listed in panel scope.

---

TYPE OF PROCEDURE / TEST

Comprehensive Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer (HBOC) Multi-Gene Panel
Panel scope: 26-gene sequencing panel covering: BRCA1, BRCA2, PALB2, CHEK2, ATM, CDH1, PTEN, STK11, TP53, NBN, RAD51C, RAD51D, BARD1, BRIP1, MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2, EPCAM, MRE11, RAD50, NF1, MUTYH, APC, SMAD4, BMPR1A
Analytic sensitivity for sequence variants: >99%
Analytic sensitivity for large rearrangements/deletions: >95% (by MLPA)

---

GROSS / SPECIMEN DESCRIPTION

Two 10 mL EDTA (purple-top) tubes of peripheral venous blood received, properly labeled and at appropriate temperature. No hemolysis or clotting identified. Specimens processed within 72 hours of collection per standard protocol. Total genomic DNA extracted from buffy coat fraction. Pre-analytical quality metrics within acceptable range. No specimen processing concerns documented.

---

DIAGNOSTIC / MOLECULAR FINDINGS

PATHOGENIC VARIANT IDENTIFIED:

Gene: BRCA1 (NM_007294.4)
Variant: c.5074del (p.Cys1692ValfsTer17)
Zygosity: Heterozygous
Classification: PATHOGENIC

Variant Description:
This single-nucleotide deletion within exon 17 of BRCA1 results in a frameshift beginning at codon 1692, with introduction of a premature termination codon 17 amino acids downstream (p.Cys1692ValfsTer17). The predicted consequence is production of a truncated, non-functional BRCA1 protein and/or nonsense-mediated mRNA decay, leading to loss of BRCA1 function. This variant has been documented in multiple independent families with hereditary breast and ovarian cancer (HBOC) syndrome and is well-represented in the ClinVar database (ClinVar Accession SCV000xxxxx) as pathogenic, with review status of multiple submitters, no conflicts. The variant is also catalogued in the Breast Cancer Information Core (BIC) database as a disease-causing frameshift mutation.

The variant was confirmed by bidirectional Sanger sequencing of the relevant amplicon.

Allele frequency in population databases: Absent from gnomAD v3.1 (0/251,292 alleles) and from the ExAC database, consistent with a rare, disease-associated germline variant rather than a benign polymorphism.

Sanger Confirmation: CONFIRMED (bidirectional Sanger sequencing performed on independent DNA aliquot)

---

ADDITIONAL GENES ANALYZED -- NEGATIVE FINDINGS:

All remaining 25 genes on the panel were analyzed by both NGS sequence analysis and MLPA large rearrangement analysis. No pathogenic variants (Class 5) or likely pathogenic variants (Class 4) were identified in any of the following genes:

BRCA2: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected. No large rearrangements detected.
PALB2: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected.
CHEK2: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected. (Note: c.1100delC founder variant specifically interrogated -- not detected.)
ATM: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected.
CDH1: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected.
PTEN: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected. No large rearrangements detected.
STK11: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected.
TP53: No germline pathogenic variants detected. (Note: Somatic TP53 p.R248W mutation identified on tumor NGS is distinct from germline and is not detected in blood-based DNA, confirming somatic origin.)
NBN: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected.
RAD51C: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected. (Note: RAD51C promoter methylation, identified on tumor NGS, represents an epigenetic somatic event and is not assessable by germline sequencing.)
RAD51D: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected.
BARD1: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected.
BRIP1: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected.
MLH1: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected. No large rearrangements detected. (MLH1 promoter methylation not assessed on germline platform.)
MSH2: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected. No large rearrangements detected.
MSH6: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected.
PMS2: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected.
EPCAM: No large deletions detected by MLPA.
MRE11: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected.
RAD50: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected.
NF1: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected.
MUTYH: No biallelic pathogenic variants detected. One heterozygous variant of uncertain significance (VUS) identified: MUTYH c.857G>A (p.Gly286Glu) -- see Variants of Uncertain Significance section below.
APC: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected.
SMAD4: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected.
BMPR1A: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants detected.

---

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE (VUS):

Gene: MUTYH
Variant: c.857G>A (p.Gly286Glu), heterozygous
Classification: Variant of Uncertain Significance (Class 3)
Clinical significance: At this time, this variant does not meet criteria for pathogenic or likely pathogenic classification. MUTYH-associated polyposis (MAP) is an autosomal recessive condition; a single heterozygous MUTYH variant is not expected to significantly increase cancer risk in the absence of a second pathogenic variant on the other allele. No second pathogenic MUTYH variant detected on this panel. Clinical significance of this heterozygous VUS alone is uncertain. Reclassification may occur as additional evidence accumulates; this laboratory will provide variant reclassification notification per standard policy.

Note: This VUS does not change the primary clinical interpretation of this report.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES:

MLP Analysis (Large Rearrangements): COMPLETED. No large deletions or duplications involving BRCA1 or BRCA2 detected. (Note: The pathogenic BRCA1 c.5074del is a small frameshift deletion, not a large rearrangement; it is detected by sequencing and confirmed by Sanger, not MLPA.)

Sanger Sequencing Confirmation: COMPLETED for BRCA1 c.5074del pathogenic variant -- CONFIRMED heterozygous.

Fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH): Not indicated for germline sequencing.

RNA analysis: Not performed. RNA-based splicing analysis pending only if reclassification of any VUS is under consideration -- currently not indicated.

Family Cascade Testing: Not performed on this specimen. Cascade genetic testing for first-degree relatives is recommended and can be arranged through the Genetics department upon request. Testing of identified family members may use a targeted single-variant assay for BRCA1 c.5074del at substantially reduced cost and turnaround time.

---

INTERPRETATION

A PATHOGENIC germline variant in BRCA1 is identified in this patient: c.5074del (p.Cys1692ValfsTer17), heterozygous.

This result is diagnostic of Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer (HBOC) Syndrome (OMIM #604370). Germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant carriers face substantially elevated lifetime risks of breast cancer, contralateral breast cancer, and epithelial ovarian cancer (including fallopian tube and primary peritoneal carcinomas) compared to the general population. Estimated BRCA1 carrier lifetime risks based on current literature include:

-- Breast cancer (cumulative to age 80): approximately 72% (95% CI 65-79%)
-- Ovarian cancer (cumulative to age 80): approximately 44-46%
-- Contralateral breast cancer: substantially elevated above background (10-year risk estimated 20-30% following first breast cancer diagnosis)
-- Pancreatic cancer: modestly elevated
-- Melanoma: potentially modestly elevated

The identification of a somatic BRCA1 frameshift mutation (p.V1687fs, VAF 45%) on the patient's tumor NGS report, distinct from but in the same gene as the germline variant, is consistent with somatic loss of heterozygosity (LOH) at the BRCA1 locus in the tumor -- a phenomenon expected when the remaining wild-type allele is somatically inactivated. This pattern of biallelic BRCA1 inactivation (one germline allele, one somatic allele) in the tumor is consistent with BRCA1 as a classic tumor suppressor gene and further corroborates the pathogenicity of the germline variant.

Additionally, RAD51C promoter methylation detected on tumor NGS represents a somatic epigenetic event and does not indicate a germline RAD51C alteration. Germline RAD51C sequencing on this panel is negative.

Note on TP53: The somatic TP53 p.R248W gain-of-function mutation identified on tumor NGS was NOT detected in germline blood DNA. This confirms somatic origin of the TP53 mutation. Li-Fraumeni syndrome due to germline TP53 is excluded.

---

QUALITY METRICS

Mean sequencing depth across panel: 412x
Minimum depth at any reportable position: 68x
Percent bases covered at >=20x: 99.97%
Sample call rate: 99.98%
All quality control metrics within laboratory-specified acceptable ranges.

---

FINAL DIAGNOSIS

HEREDITARY BREAST AND OVARIAN CANCER (HBOC) SYNDROME
Germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant: c.5074del (p.Cys1692ValfsTer17), heterozygous
All other 25 genes on panel: No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants identified
Variant of Uncertain Significance: MUTYH c.857G>A (p.Gly286Glu), heterozygous (not clinically actionable as single heterozygous VUS)

---

REPORTING PATHOLOGIST / LABORATORY DIRECTOR

Director, Molecular Genetics Laboratory
Board-Certified, Clinical Molecular Genetics and Genomics (ABMGG)

Electronic signature applied upon final review and authorization.

Report Status: FINAL

---

NOTE: This report is intended for use by a qualified healthcare provider in the context of a comprehensive clinical evaluation. Genetic counseling is an integral component of the appropriate use of hereditary cancer genetic testing results. Results should be interpreted in conjunction with personal and family history. This laboratory participates in variant reclassification and will issue amended reports if variant classifications change.
